title: Some Aspects of the Conduct and Mode of Life of the Deposed Muhammad b. Harun
description: Deposed Muhammad b. Hdrun762 According to Humayd b. Said, who said: After he became ruler and after al-Ma'mun wrote to him and gave him his allegiance, Muhammad sought out eunuchs and purchased them, spending inordinately on them. He appointed them to [attend on] his private quarters by night and by day, his provisions of food and drink, and his decisions commanding or forbidding.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 31
